LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MOUNT DIABLO INVESTMENT GROUP'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS UNDER CCP 128.7 AGAINST LOANVEST IX, LP, LOANVEST XII AND MARK RUSHIN IS GRANTED IN LARGE PART. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE AND THE LOANVEST ENTITIES AND MT. RUSHIN ARE REQUIRED TO PAY $1,270 TO MT. DIABLO FOR THE REASONABLE FEES AND COSTS (4 HOURS OF ATTORNEY WORK AT $295 PER HOUR PLUS $90 IN COSTS) IT INCURRED IN FILING THIS MOTION. ORDER AND JUDGMENT [OF DISMISSAL] SIGNED. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-09-2018 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-31-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Hannah R. Salassi, Esq. of Lvovich & Szucsko PC, T:415.392.2560 ext 114, for defendant/moving party;
Mark Rushin, Esq., T:415.279.5271, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MOUNT DIABLO INVESTMENT GROUP LLC, et al.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT filed by plaintiffs Loanvest IX, LP and Loanvest XII, LP is off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on Mt. Diablo's motion for sanctions.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Under 128.7 Against Loanvest, Xii, Llp, And Mark Rushin As Attorney Of Record
Off Calendar Jan-31-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Hannah R. Salassi, Esq. of Lvovich & Szucsko PC, T:415.392.2560 ext 114, for defendant/moving party;
Mark Rushin, Esq., T:415.279.5271, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MOUNT DIABLO INVESTMENT GROUP'S Motion For Sanctions Under CCP 128.7 Against Loanvest IX, LP, Loanvest XII and Mark Rushin is granted in large part. The first amended complaint is dismissed with prejudice and the Loanvest entities and Mt. Rushin are required to pay $1,270 to Mt. Diablo for the reasonable fees and costs (4 hours of attorney work at $295 per hour plus $90 in costs) it incurred in filing this motion. The first amended complaint and all causes of action therein filed by Mr. Rushin on behalf of the Loanvest entities are frivolous in that they are not warranted by law or a nonfrivolous extension of law and they lack any evidentiary support. All of the causes of action are barred by the settlement agreement in the San Mateo lawsuit. The assertion of the Loanvest entities and Mr. Rushin that the Loanvest entities are not "affiliates" of South Bay Real Estate Group, LLC is disingenuous and wholly lacking in any admissible factual support. Mr. Rushin's statements in his declaration regarding his unexpressed intentions about the scope of the settlement agreement are inadmissible. There is no dispute, nor could there credibly be such a dispute, that South Bay had a significant relationship with the Loanvest entities at the time of the settlement agreement that makes it equally indisputable that those entities were affiliates of South Bay. Moreover, while not necessary to resolution of this motion, it is also clear that the allegations in the first amended complaint that the settlement monies were to be paid to the Loanvest entities are likewise frivolous.

ORDER AND JUDGMENT [OF DISMISSAL] SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.
